channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Big March storm fans wildfires and kills 3 as forecasters fear weekend tornado outbreak
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — A sprawling storm system crossing the U.S. on Friday caused deadly crashes, damaged buildings and fanned more than 100 wildfires in several central states, prompting evacuation orders in some communities.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY and RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democrats confront limits of their minority power after bruising shutdown vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Democrats were grim Friday as they left Washington after a brutal 10-week stretch that consistently showed the limits of their power in the minority — and culminated with a deeply personal rupture over how to best counter President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Oklahoma man says Social Security benefits terminated without warning or explanation
item.description: A local retiree says his Social Security benefits were suddenly suspended without warning — and with no explanation given when he reached out. He worries it may have to do with the place he was born, and ongoing DOGE cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Humphrey/KFOR, Fox 5

item.title: Doechii joins Will Smith to recreate viral TikTok trend
item.description: The trend uses her song "Anxiety" and spoofs a scene from "The Fresh Prince of Bell-Air" where Smith's character sneaks up on Ali's, who's dancing with her headphones on, and starts to mimic her.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, KRON4

item.title: Senate approves funding bill hours before shutdown deadline, sending to Trump for signature
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate passed a Republican-led spending bill Friday hours before a government shutdown, overcoming sharp Democratic opposition to the measure and sending it to President Donald Trump to be signed into law.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump pledges to 'expose' his enemies in political speech at Justice Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump pledged to “expose” his enemies during a norm-breaking political speech Friday at the Justice Department in which he aired a litany of grievances about the criminal cases he faced and vowed retribution for what he described as the “lies and abuses that have o
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate votes to avoid government shutdown
item.description: The Senate voted mostly along party lines Friday afternoon to pass the House Republican-drafted bill to fund government through September, avoiding a government shutdown only hours before funding was due to lapse. President Trump is expected to sign the bill into law. The final vote was 54-46.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: More than 50 universities face federal investigations as part of Trump's anti-DEI campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 50 universities are being investigated for alleged racial discrimination as part of President Donald Trump’s campaign to end diversity, equity and inclusion programs that his officials say exclude white and Asian American students.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most disapprove of Trump's approach to Ukraine, Russia: Survey
item.description: Nearly six in 10 people, 59 percent, said it was “not too" or “not at all likely” that Trump’s approach to the Russia-Ukraine war will bring long-term peace between the two Eastern European countries. Around 41 percent disagreed.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Viral 'American Idol' contestant dead at 32, family says
item.description: Douglas Kiker, the garbage collector who touched hearts with his singing voice on "American Idol" has died, his family announced on social media. He was 32 years old.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: Helicopters permanently restricted on route near Washington airport where 67 died in midair crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Helicopters will be permanently restricted from flying near Washington, D.C.'s airport on the same route where a passenger jet and an Army helicopter collided in midair, killing 67 people, the Federal Aviation Administration said Friday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and TARA COPP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DJ Daniel sworn in as honorary police officer more than 800 times
item.description: He has a rare brain and spine cancer with no cure. His father, Theodis Daniel, says DJ fights for his life daily.
item.dc:creator: Myriam Samake, NewsNation

item.title: Cause of death for Gene Hackman and Betsy Arakawa's dog released
item.description: An examination of the dog found dead along with actor Gene Hackman and his wife in their Santa Fe home shows dehydration and starvation were likely what led to the animal’s demise.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Protestant denominations try new ideas as they face declines in members and money
item.description: When the Episcopal Church recently announced cuts to its national staff, it was the latest in a long-running cycle among historic U.S. Protestant denominations — declines in members leading to declines in funding and thus in staff. And it wasn't alone. The Presbyterian Church (U.S.A.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Measles confirmed in 3 more states as cases multiply
item.description: More than 300 cases of measles, one of the most contagious viruses in the world, have now been confirmed across more than a dozen U.S. states, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Voice of America ending contracts with Associated Press, Reuters and Agence France Presse
item.description: The government agency that runs the Voice of America has moved to terminate contracts with The Associated Press, Reuters and Agence France-Presse, and told its journalists Friday to stop using material from the wire services.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DHS agents search Columbia dorm rooms after Khalil arrest
item.description: Department of Homeland Security agents searched two dorm rooms at Columbia University Thursday night, the school said, a move that comes less than a week after the federal arrest of former grad student Mahmoud Khalil.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Trump demands unprecedented control at Columbia, alarming scholars and speech groups
item.description: The Trump administration has delivered an extraordinary ultimatum to Columbia University, threatening to permanently end federal funding to the school unless it cedes control of an international studies department and implements sweeping changes to other policies.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Arrest made after Austin, Texas 17-vehicle crash leaves 3 adults, 2 kids dead
item.description: Police in Austin, Texas arrested a suspect Friday following a 17-vehicle crash on I-35 that left five people dead, including a child and an infant.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 12 taken to hospital with minor injuries after plane catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, WGN 9

item.title: American Airlines Boeing 737 catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 60 percent of voters unhappy with DOGE handling of federal workers: Poll
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE have been trying to slash their way through the federal government, offering buyouts to and laying off scores of workers. Many of their actions are now the subject of lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Luigi Mangione's defense fund raises over $722K
item.description: "People are raising money not for his innocence but because they're in support of allegedly what he did.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, KTLA 5

item.title: ICE Boston arrests Guatemalan national charged with child rape who was released back into community by court
item.description: Guatemalan national, Stivenson Omar Perez-Ajtzalan, 19, was arrested on Jan. 30 for aggravated rape of a child 10 years younger than him, according to ICE Boston.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Once-routine immigration check-ins become high-stakes calculation as some are detained
item.description: When a couple from Colombia who was planning their wedding showed up for a check-in with U.S. immigration authorities, one was given his next appointment date. The other was detained and deported. Jhojan doesn’t know why Felipe was detained at the Feb.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, CEDAR ATTANASIO and SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Founding girl group member reveals she's been living in her car
item.description: "If you would have said to me while I was En Vogue, 'You're going to be living in your car one day,' I'd be like, 'Huh?"
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: VIDEO: American Airlines plane evacuated in Denver after fire aboard
item.description: Passengers were evacuated Thursday evening from an American Airlines flight after a fire was reported.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, ABC 4

item.title: Columbia announces disciplinary action for students who took over campus building
item.description: The discipline includes “multi-year suspensions, temporary degree revocations, and expulsions,” according to the school.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Columbine victim's death from health problems related to 1999 school shooting ruled a homicide
item.description: DENVER (AP) — The death of a woman who was partially paralyzed in the Columbine High School shooting has been ruled a homicide, raising the death toll of the 1999 attack to 14. Anne Marie Hochhalter died Feb.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Capri Sun launches glow-in-the-dark Moon Punch for lunar eclipse
item.description: Capri Sun has launched its first-ever glow-in-the-dark pouch, known as Moon Pouch, in honor of the upcoming Total Lunar Eclipse. The total lunar eclipse, also known as a Blood Moon, occurs when the Earth is fully positioned between the sun and the moon.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Putin agrees in principle with proposal for Ukraine ceasefire and says more discussions are needed
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Thursday that he agrees in principle with a U.S. proposal for a 30-day ceasefire in Ukraine, but he emphasized that the terms are yet to be worked out and added that any truce should pave the way to lasting peace.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to partly allow birthright citizenship restrictions
item.description: The Trump administration is asking the Supreme Court to allow restrictions on birthright citizenship to partly take effect while legal fights play out.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Third MLB hat pulled over accidental vulgarity as others sell for hundreds online
item.description: Yet another newly released MLB hat has been pulled from online stores after fans noted the potentially crass design the cap sported.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: FAFSA site goes down after mass layoff at Education Department
item.description: The Studentaid.gov website was down for hours on Wednesday after the Education Department laid off half its workforce.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dem AGs sue over Trump administration’s Education Department layoffs
item.description: Democratic attorneys general in Washington, D.C., and 20 states sued Thursday over the Trump administration’s efforts to lay off nearly half of the Education Department workforce.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Trump threatens Europe with 200 percent tariff on wine, Champagne
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday threatened to put a hefty 200 percent tariff on wine and Champagne coming from Europe if the European Union (EU) does not remove a tariff on whisky.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about US student Sudiksha Konanki, who vanished in Dominican Republic on spring break
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — Investigators continue to search for a college student from Virginia who vanished earlier this month while visiting the Dominican Republic with five other people during spring break from classes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
